channel.title: CloudJourneyBlog
channel.description: Security, automation, and the systems that connect them.

item.title: Zero Trust Rollout Retrospective: What Actually Broke
item.description: A multi-year Zero Trust program across MDE, Intune baselines, Conditional Access, phishing-resistant MFA, 802.1X, and MDI. Here's what we implemented, in what order, and what broke first.

item.title: Forcing the Shadow IT Migration: Making Personal Gmail Unusable in a Managed Network
item.description: Personal Gmail inside your corporate network isn't just a compliance risk — it's a data leak waiting to happen. Here's how to use CASB policies, Context-Aware Access, and structured migration to make shadow IT unusable.

item.title: Google Workspace to Entra ID Federation: The SSO Setup That Actually Works
item.description: Setting up Google Workspace to use Entra ID as the Identity Provider isn't just checking a box. Here's the practical walkthrough of SAML federation, claims mapping, and what breaks when you get it wrong.

item.title: The Entra Join Readiness Checklist: Everything You Must Resolve Before Cutting the Domain Cord
item.description: Moving from hybrid Azure AD join to Entra-only join isn't a flip-a-switch decision. Here's the practical checklist of what to resolve before you cut the domain cord — and the decision framework for wipe-and-reload versus in-place migration.

item.title: Automating HR Lifecycle Events for a Growing Veterinary Marketplace
item.description: Building a set of automated HR-driven workflows for a fast-growing client — onboarding, role changes, and offboarding orchestrated through HiBob and a custom middleware layer.

item.title: Spinning Up a Client's Production Environment: Azure WAF + VPS
item.description: Setting up a new hosting environment from scratch — the decisions, trade-offs, and lessons from provisioning infrastructure for a client website on Azure.

item.title: The Robot Army: My Personal Lab on Azure
item.description: How I built a personal automation platform on Azure — a VPS behind a WAF, Docker Swarm for orchestration, and n8n as the brain that ties everything together.

item.title: Building a Zero Trust Program: Lessons from a Large Energy Sector Deployment
item.description: What we actually did — and what broke — rolling out a full Zero Trust program across a large energy sector organization with 57 tasks across the full stack.

item.title: AI-Driven SOC: Layering Intelligence on Top of What You Already Own
item.description: Most SOC teams don't need a new platform — they need the tools they already have to work together smarter. Here's how to build an AI-assisted security operations capability using Wazuh, Microsoft Defender, and lightweight automation.

item.title: Cross-Platform Compliance Architecture: Building a Kill-Switch Enforcement System
item.description: Most compliance frameworks treat enforcement as a checkbox. Here's how to build an actual cross-platform enforcement system that can kill access the moment a device goes non-compliant — Windows, macOS, and Linux.

item.title: Claude Code Config v3: LTM as Infrastructure
item.description: The config got a significant overhaul: SQLite-backed long-term memory, a dedicated MCP server exposing LTM primitives, semantic recall with embedding fallback, graph-based reasoning, and an EvaluateSession hook that extracts decisions automatically.

item.title: Building a Device Inventory Pipeline
item.description: When your MDM doesn't tell the full story, build the pipeline yourself. Here's the Jenkins → PowerShell → SharePoint → Power BI architecture I built to unify device data across AD and Intune for a FinTech client.

item.title: When HiBob Is Your Source of Truth
item.description: Your HR system knows the org better than your IdP does. Here's how I built a bi-directional HiBob ↔ Entra ID sync that keeps departments, titles, managers, and 15 other attributes in sync — automatically.

item.title: The JumpCloud Exit Playbook
item.description: Migrating 200+ Windows and macOS devices from JumpCloud to Microsoft Intune without breaking MDM coverage, compliance posture, or user productivity.

item.title: Phishing-Resistant MFA Rollout
item.description: Standard MFA is no longer enough. Here's how we designed a gradual Conditional Access rollout to enforce FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, and Platform SSO across a security-conscious OT environment.

item.title: Smart Offboarding Orchestration
item.description: Manual offboarding leaves doors open — sometimes for days. Here's how I automated a 15-step enterprise offboarding workflow using Azure Logic Apps and Automation Runbooks triggered by an HR webhook.

item.title: The Claude Code Workflow That Actually Stuck
item.description: I started with a simple 4-step loop and got the best results I'd ever seen from an AI tool. Then I extended it. Here's what changed, what didn't, and why starting with structure beats starting from scratch.

item.title: Claude Code Config v2: SQLite LTM, a Janitor Agent, and a Memory Graph
item.description: The markdown-file memory system worked until it didn't. Here's what I replaced it with — a SQLite LTM, an autonomous Janitor agent, and a D3 graph that shows you what the model actually knows.

item.title: From Script to System: How I Turned a Jenkins Pipeline into FleetWatch
item.description: My client wanted PowerShell scripts dumping Intune data into SharePoint for Power BI. I built a web app instead. Here's why — and how.

item.title: Netlogon RPC Sealing: The Full Hardening Playbook
item.description: A complete 4-phase execution guide for enforcing Netlogon RPC Sealing and SMB/NTLM hardening before migrating to Entra ID — including the PowerShell discovery script I use to find vulnerable traffic across all DCs.

item.title: How I Configured Claude Code to Actually Remember Things
item.description: The default Claude Code setup loses context on every session. Here's the hook system, skill commands, agent definitions, and memory architecture I built to fix that.

item.title: My Homelab on Docker Swarm: What Runs, How It's Wired, What Broke
item.description: A tour of my self-hosted infrastructure — Docker Swarm for orchestration, Traefik for ingress, Infisical for secrets, and the modular design that replaced the monolith I should never have written.

item.title: What I Actually Learned from Vibe Coding with Claude Code
item.description: A year of building projects with AI: what the workflow looks like, how I keep context alive across sessions, which safety patterns actually work, and what breaks if you skip them.

item.title: Zero Trust Is Not a Product
item.description: Every vendor sells 'Zero Trust.' Most of what they're selling is a marketing label on a firewall. Here's what the model actually means and why it matters.

item.title: Conditional Access in Practice
item.description: Conditional Access is where Zero Trust principles actually land in a Microsoft environment. Here's how to think about it, what to get right, and where I've seen it go wrong.

item.title: Endpoint Hardening as a Zero Trust Control
item.description: A compliant device in Conditional Access is only meaningful if your compliance policies check things that actually matter. Here's what to harden and how to measure it.

item.title: Zero Trust for AI Agents
item.description: AI agents have access to real tools — APIs, databases, shell execution. The same principles that apply to human users apply here, and most agentic systems ignore them.

item.title: Identity-First Security
item.description: The network perimeter is gone. Identity is what's left. Here's what building security around identity looks like in practice and why it changes almost everything.
